'''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。 {{神社 |画像= |名称='''畝尾坐健土安神社''' |所在地=奈良県橿原市下八釣町138 |祭神=[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]] |神体= |社格=[[式内社]]（大社） |例祭=9月28日}}

旧[[村社]] |本殿=流造 |例祭=9月28日}} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。 ==歴史== [[伊邪那美]]が火の神の[[迦具土命]]を産んで[[陰部]]に[[火傷]]を負い、亡くなった。この時に、尿や糞、嘔吐物から新たな神々を産んだ。その糞から産まれた神が祭神の[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]]である。 {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおにますたけはにやすしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

旧[[村社]] |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[9月28日]]}} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。 ==歴史== [[伊邪那美]]が火の神の[[迦具土命]]を産んで[[陰部]]に[[火傷]]を負い、亡くなった。この時に、尿や糞、嘔吐物から新たな神々を産んだ。その糞から産まれた神が祭神の[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]]である。 {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおにますたけはにやすしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年5月7日 (日) 10:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[Image:DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |名称='''畝尾坐健土安神社''' |所在地=奈良県橿原市下八釣町138 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]] |神体= |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[9月28日]]}} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。 ==歴史== 『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀己未年二月二十日の条に
「天皇、前年の秋九月を以て、 潜に天香山の埴土を取りて、八十の平瓫を造りて、躬自ら斎戒して諸神を祭りたまふ。遂に区宇を安定むること得たまふ。故、土を取りし処を号けて、埴土と白ふ」
という埴安の地に祀られた土霊が当社であるという。
『大和志』では「今称天照大神」とあり、近世に天照大神社と称していた。 ==祭神== [[伊邪那美]]が火の神の[[迦具土命]]を産んで[[陰部]]に[[火傷]]を負い、亡くなった。この時に、尿や糞、嘔吐物から新たな神々を産んだ。その糞から産まれた神が祭神の[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]]である。 {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおにますたけはにやすしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

「天皇、前年の秋九月を以て、 潜に天香山の埴土を取りて、八十の平瓫を造りて、躬自ら斎戒して諸神を祭りたまふ。遂に区宇を安定むること得たまふ。故、土を取りし処を号けて、埴土と白ふ」
という埴安の地に祀られた土霊が当社であるという。
『大和志』では「今称天照大神」とあり、近世に天照大神社と称していた。 ==祭神== [[伊邪那美]]が火の神の[[迦具土命]]を産んで[[陰部]]に[[火傷]]を負い、亡くなった。この時に、尿や糞、嘔吐物から新たな神々を産んだ。その糞から産まれた神が祭神の[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]]である。 {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおにますたけはにやすしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年5月7日 (日) 10:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[Image:DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |名称='''畝尾坐健土安神社''' |所在地=[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]下八釣町138 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]] |神体= |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[9月28日]]}} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。隣接する南西の位置に同じく式内社の[[畝尾都多本神社]]が鎮座している。 ==歴史== 『[[日本書紀]]』神武天皇即位前紀己未年二月二十日の条に
「天皇、前年の秋九月を以て、 潜に天香山の埴土を取りて、八十平瓮を造りて、躬自ら[[物忌み|斎戒]]して諸神を祭りたまふ。遂に区宇を安定むること得たまふ。故、土を取りし処を号けて、埴土と白ふ」
という埴安の地に祀られた土霊が当社であるという。
『大和志』では「今称天照大神」とあり、近世に天照大神社と称していた。 ==祭神== [[伊邪那美]]が火の神の[[迦具土命]]を産んで[[陰部]]に[[火傷]]を負い、亡くなった。この時に、尿や糞、嘔吐物から新たな神々を産んだ。その糞から産まれた神が祭神の[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]]である。 {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおにますたけはにやすしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年5月7日 (日) 10:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[Image:DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |名称='''畝尾坐健土安神社''' |所在地=[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]下八釣町138 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[波邇夜須毘売神|健土安比売命]] |神体= |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[9月28日]]}} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。隣接する南西の位置に同じく式内社の[[畝尾都多本神社]]が鎮座している。1446年(文安3年)の『和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解』には[[天香山神社 | 天香山坐櫛真命神社]]・坂門神社(天岩戸神社)・[[畝尾坐健土安神社]]とともに、「天香山坐四處神社」と称している[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm 畝尾都多本神社]。 ==歴史== 『[[日本書紀]]』神武天皇即位前紀己未年二月二十日の条に
「天皇、前年の秋九月を以て、 潜に天香山の埴土を取りて、八十平瓮を造りて、躬自ら[[物忌み|斎戒]]して諸神を祭りたまふ。遂に区宇を安定むること得たまふ。故、土を取りし処を号けて、埴土と白ふ」

旧[[村社]] |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[9月28日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=畝尾坐健土安神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''畝尾坐健土安神社'''（うねおにますたけはにやすじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある神社である。[[天香具山]]の北西麓に鎮座している。隣接する南西の位置に同じく式内社の[[畝尾都多本神社]]が鎮座している。1446年(文安3年)の『和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解』には[[天香山神社 | 天香山坐櫛真命神社]]・坂門神社(天岩戸神社)・[[畝尾都多本神社]]とともに、「天香山坐四處神社」と称している[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm 畝尾都多本神社]。 ==歴史== 『[[日本書紀]]』神武天皇即位前紀己未年二月二十日の条に

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |image=[[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location={{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命}} |shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |main hall={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |annual festival={{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinai-sha]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]]

He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinai-sha]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}

{{Cite sources|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |image=[[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location={{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命}} |shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |main hall={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |annual festival={{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinai-sha]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}

It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinai-sha]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

{{Cite sources|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |image=[[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location={{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命}} |shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |main hall={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |annual festival={{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinai-sha]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

{{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |image= |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34|29|57|N|135|48|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara�]]

It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Yamato Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30931377]] Wikidata name: Uneoni masutakehayaniyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34.49916666666667|N|135.81555555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30931377]] Wikidata name: Uneoni masutakehayaniyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34.49916666666667|N|135.81555555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30931377]] Wikidata name: Uneoni masutakehayaniyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34.49916666666667|N|135.81555555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}}

[[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30931377]] Wikidata name: Uneoni masutakehayaniyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34.49916666666667|N|135.81555555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|qq=|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}}

According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30931377]] Wikidata name: Uneoni masutakehayaniyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 7, 2017 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg |name='''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' |location=[[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{Coord|34.49916666666667|N|135.81555555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|健土安比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}}

Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival=[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the pre-ascension records of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}
