It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |main hall={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |annual festival={{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28|1=September 28}} |map={{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture={{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} }} '''Uneo ni Masu Takehaniyasu Shrine''' (畝尾坐健土安神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is situated at the northwestern foothills of {{ill|Mount Amanokagu|ja|天香具山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Amanokagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香久山|1=Mount Amanokagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443780}}. To the southwest, adjacent to the shrine, stands another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}. In the year 1446 (Bunan 3), the shrine was recorded in the ''Washū Five Districts Shrine Registry'' (和州五郡神社神名帳大略注解) as one of the "Four Shrines of Amanokagu" (天香山坐四處神社), alongside {{ill|Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja|天香山坐櫛真命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Kaguyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja|坂門神社 (天岩戸神社)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakado Shrine (Amano-Iwato Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|ja|畝尾都多本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine|1=Uneotsutamoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28685121}}[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneo Tsutamoto Shrine]. == History == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the pre-ascension records of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, under the entry for the 20th day of the second month of the year ''Kinoe-Mi'' (己未), it is stated: ''"In the ninth month of the previous autumn, the Emperor secretly took sacred clay from Mount Amanokagu and crafted eighty ''hirakame'' (sacred earthenware). He then purified himself and performed a ritual to the gods, thereby securing the stability of the land. Because of this, the place where the clay was gathered was named 'Haniyasu'"''. It is believed that the deity enshrined here is the spirit of the land itself, which was revered in Haniyasu. According to the ''{{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'', the shrine was once referred to as "Amaterasu-Ōmikami" (天照大神), and during the Edo period, it was called "Amaterasu Shrine" (天照大神社). == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|波邇夜須毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}, a goddess born from the excrement of {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, who suffered burns and perished after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. It is said that from her bodily fluids—urine, feces, and vomit—various deities were born, with Haniyasu-hime emerging from her feces. == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|畝尾坐健土安神社|version=89792459|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:畝尾坐健土安神社拝殿 - DGYGae0UIAABNnZ.jpg|280px]] [[ja:畝尾坐健土安神社]] {{wikidata link|Q30931377}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
